title: Release Gates, Incident Drills, Rollback, and Residual-Risk Defense · Language Lens: Rollback Containment
summary: A successful demonstration becomes release approval despite open owner, parser, accessibility, capacity, recovery, and incident questions. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go rollback containment increment in Go crawler production defense dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Stop effects first, preserve evidence, revoke authority, restore known artifact and state, validate offline, resume narrowly, and document corrections.
Rehearse owner revoke, runaway frontier, private redirect, credential leak, parser drift, inaccessible report, goroutine leak, corrupt checkpoint, and unsafe output.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Stop effects first, preserve evidence, revoke authority, restore known artifact and state, validate offline, resume narrowly, and document corrections.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Rollback Containment model · Theory Rollback Containment, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Rollback Containment precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Rollback Containment model · Theory Rollback Containment, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory Go team handles case 2267 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend release matrices, end-to-end counts, incident drills, rollback containment, non-claims, and risks; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Stop effects first, preserve evidence, revoke authority, restore known artifact and state, validate offline, resume narrowly, and document corrections.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Rollback Containment model · Theory Rollback Containment, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Deploying prior binary immediately restores safe operation.

checks.description: The cumulative go portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-go-rollback-containment-s71gjq.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-go-rollback-containment-s71gjq(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_crawler_go_rollback_containment_s71gjq\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?releaseGates < 24)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?passedGates < 23 \|\| passedGates > releaseGates)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?incidentDrills < 10)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?openCriticalRisks > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using stop and effects plus specific case evidence.
